english: skyod-na (skyod na) when the wind agitates the branches Dzl.; to shake; hence Mi-skyód-pa (mi skyod pa), Akshobhya, n. of the second Dhyani-Buddha. — 2. W.: resp. to go, to walk, (= gśégs-pa (gshegs pa), byón-pa (byon pa) B. C.) *ṅaṅ-du skyod* (*ngang du skyod*) step in, if you please! — 3. W.: to go down, to set, of the sun, moon etc., to expire, to pass, to elapse, of time.

1. fault, defect (opp. to yon-tan (yon tan)), skyon gaṅ yaṅ med (skyon gang yang med) I have not to complain of anything, I do not want anything Dzl.; damage, harm, disadvantage, misfortune, khrul-pa-la skyon ći yod ('khrul pa la skyon ci yod) what harm is there in erring? Thgy.; C.: *mi kyoṇ, kyoṇ me'* (*mi kyon, kyon me'*), no harm, no matter (W. more freq.: *mi sto* (*mi sto*)); gźán-gyi skyon tós-na dgá-ba (gzhan gyi skyon thos na dga' ba) rejoicing in the calamities of others, malicious Glr.; skyón-du mtóṅ-ba (skyon du mthong ba) to consider it a loss Glr. — 2. bodily defect, fault, as lameness; derangement, disorder in the mixture of the humours Med. — 3. spiritual defect, sin, vicious quality, rdzún-du smrá-bai skyon (rdzun du smra ba'i skyon) the sin of lying Dzl.; skyón-gyis ma gos (skyon gyis ma gos) not defiled by sin; lar skyon če (lar skyon che) but that is very bad (of you) Glr.; skyon byéd-pa (skyon byed pa) Cs. to commit a fault, sél-ba (sel ba) Lex. to remove, amend, correct a fault, spáṅ-ba (spang ba) to leave off, to quit it; mi-la skyon bébs-pa (mi la skyon 'bebs pa), dógs-pa ('dogs pa) (col. *tág-pa, tág-če* (*btags pa, btags ce*)) to charge one with a crime, to calumniate Glr.; gźán-gyi skyon gléṅ-ba (gzhan gyi skyon gleng ba), rjód-pa (rjod pa), to name the faults of others, to speak ill of them, to slander B., C., Schr. also: to blame, criticise. — skyón-čan (skyon can) 1. faulty, defective, incorrect, e.g. dag-yig (dag yig) the spelling of a word. 2. sinful, subject to vice. — 4. symb. num: 18.

pf. (b)skyon ((b)skyon) to put astride upon a thing, (causative form to źón-pa (zhon pa)), mi źig rtá-la (mi zhig rta la) (or rtá-ru (rta ru)) to cause a man to mount, to go on horseback: to fix something on a stick; mi źig gsál-śiṅ-la (mi zhig gsal shing la) to empale a man.

pf. (b)skyabs ((b)skyabs), ft. bskyab (bskyab), imp. skyób(s) (skyob(s)) to protect, defend, preserve, save frq., ǰígs-pa-las ('jigs pa las) from fear, ǰíg-pa-las ('jig pa las) from destruction; bskyáb-pa (bskyab pa) the protecting power, the preserving cause Mil. (ni f.).
help, assistance, seldom for skyabs (skyabs); skyóbs-ma (skyobs ma) Thgy. id.; *śrog-kyób* (*srog skyob*) col. preservation of life, escape; also: he that saves another's life, helper.

pf. bskyoms (bskyoms), ft. bskyom (bskyom), imp. skyom(s) (skyom(s)) Cs.: to shake, agitate, stir up. Lexx. give: ču skyóm-pa (chu skyom pa) and snód skyom-pa (snod skyom pa), to stir the water, to shake a vessel.
= kyor (kyor), the hollow of the hand filled with a fluid, e.g. ču-skyór (chu skyor) a handful of water.

I. vb. pf. & ft. bskyar (bskyar) 1. to hold up, to prop, — 2. to paste. — 2. to repeat, bskyár-te btaṅ (bskyar te btang) it was repeatedly sent Dzl.; to repeat word for word what the teacher says, in order to learn it by heart Mil.; to say over again; to recite by heart (opp. to sgróg-pa (sgrog pa) to read); glu de rjes skyór-nas ma bláṅs-na (glu de rjes skyor nas ma blangs na) if one does not sing the hymn afterwards repeatedly Mil.; *kyor jaṅ jhé'-pa* (*kyor jang jhe' pa*) C. to practise repeatedly. II. sbst. enclosure, fence.

resp. dbu-skrá (dbu skra) (C.: *ṭa* (*ta*), W.: *śra** (*shra*)) the hair of the head, *śra-ló* (*shra lo*) Ld. id., used caressingly in speaking to children and women; skra daṅ k'á-spu (skra dang kha spu) the hair of the head and of the beard; skra bsgril ba (skra bsgril ba) Cs.: plaited or curled hair; skra nyag gčig (skra nyag gcig) a single hair. — skrá-čan (skra can) having long hair. — skra-do-kér (skra do ker) the hair plaited together on the crown of the head, as Buddha and Hindu-women wear it. — skra-mdúd (skra mdud) the bow of ribands at the end of the long plaits of the women in Ld. etc. — skra-tsáb (skra tshab) Cs.: false hair, a peruke. — skra-śeṅ (skra sheng) Sch. thin hair.
with instr., to be terrified, frightened by, afraid of something ǰígs-skrag-pa ('jigs skrag pa), dṅáṅs-skr. (dngangs skrag) id. B., C.
